Malicious npm Package Steals Credentials

Our researchers discovered a malicious npm package targeting developers.

Indicators of compromise

@cap-js/postgres 2.2.2

FileHash
router_init.jsab4fcadaec1d282b900de5abb5b1d55dbd0e7af9628f6e7a5e2cb0e68b3b56aa
router_init.js12ed9a3cab1b2e3d4f5a6b7c8d9e0f1a2b3c4d5e
router_init.js833fd59e4b3c2a1d5e6f7a8b9c0d1234

Network Indicators

TypeIndicator
C2 Domaingit-tanstack.com
C2 IP83.142.209.194
Payload URLhttp://git-tanstack.com/payload.sh

Additional Indicators

TypeIndicator
Service Namegh-token-monitor
macOS Persistence~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.apple.ghmonitor.plist
Linux Persistence/etc/systemd/system/gh-monitor.service
Runtime Artifacttanstack_runner.js
Path~/.vscode/extensions/tanstack.extension

Conclusion

Organizations should audit their npm dependencies immediately.
